_source: homecoming.wiki zone pages, supplied by Joel (the wiki blocks automated reads; every entry here was pasted by him).
_why: Gulbasaur's guides were written in 2020 and are excellent, but Homecoming has kept shipping zones and content since. This file is where content NEWER than the guides lives, so the road can mention it at the right level instead of quietly ending at 2020.
_ruling: Joel's standing rule keeps the wiki-bridge CLOSED for game numbers we could get from the client. These are not those: zone level ranges and TF gates are server-side and absent from every client bin (searched 2026-07-22), and he supplied these himself. Every entry is labelled as wiki-sourced on the surface — never blended into the game-first data.

zones.since: Issue 25; extensively updated in Issue 28 Page 2
zones.note: Not in either of Gulbasaur's guides — it arrived after they were written. Reachable by the Paragon City Monorail (station in Christie Square) or the Smuggler's Submarine in Old Slough. No connecting zones on foot.

events.name: Market Crash
events.note: from Ada Wellington

events.name: Marigold 'Zoe' Langston's Task Force
events.note: hero

zones.since: Revamped in Issue 22 (before that it was hero-only, levels 21-29 — that version is Echo: Dark Astoria)
zones.note: The one solo-friendly road into the Incarnate system: full story arcs at 50, not just trials. On foot through the gate in Talos Island's east wall, or by TUNNEL. Mot is free, and the whole zone is a Cold Hand of Death day-job location.

events.name: Behavioral Adjustment Facility (BAF)
events.note: Incarnate trial

events.name: Lambda Sector (LAM)
events.note: Incarnate trial

events.name: Keyes Island Reactor (KIR)
events.note: Incarnate trial

events.name: The Underground (UGT)
events.note: Incarnate trial

events.name: TPN Campus (TPN)
events.note: Incarnate trial

events.name: Minds of Mayhem (MoM)
events.note: Incarnate trial

events.name: Dilemma Diabolique (DD)
events.note: Incarnate trial

events.name: Magisterium (MAG)
events.note: Incarnate trial

zones.safe_havens_note: rune-protected, no enemies at all — somewhere to stop and breathe in a zone where everything else is 50-54

zones.note: A hazard zone: bigger spawns than the street outside, and it is walled in the middle of the early city with gates to Atlas Park, Kings Row, Skyway City and Steel Canyon (the Kings Row gate arrived in Issue 28 Page 3). The Kraken is sighted here.

events.name: Kraken
events.note: giant monster, sighted in the zone

zones.note: The reactor complex, and the way in to the Terra Volta Respecification Trials. Entered through the gate in the north wall from Independence Port. The zone's own level spread is unusually wide for its size — the neighbourhoods below are the real guide to where you belong.

Where the Hamidon raid happens. Deep in the wild reaches of Eden and wall-to-wall Devouring Earth — the wiki's own warning is that few have gone in alone and come out, so bring a league. Reached from Eden, or by the Black Helicopter Line.

events.name: Hamidon
events.note: the raid — a league, not a team

safe_havens: The abandoned Portal Corporation facility (northwest corner)
zones.safe_havens_note: relatively free of the Devouring Earth that patrol everywhere else — the one place to regroup

zones.pvp_note: Other players can attack you here. ANY level may enter and everyone is set to combat level 25 — under 25 you are sidekicked up, over 25 you are exemplared down and lose the powers you gained since. Shown at 25 because that is the level you actually fight at.
zones.note: The first of the four PvP zones. Longbow and Arachnos are both here mining the Shiva meteor fragments, and the Shiva Strike mission is how you get a Shivan pet. Reached by helicopter — Skyway City for heroes, Cap au Diable for villains.

events.name: Shiva Strike
events.note: collect meteor fragments, earn a Shivan

zones.pvp_note: Other players can attack you here. You must be at least level 20 to enter, and everyone inside is set to combat level 30 — raised or lowered. Shown at 30 because that is the level you actually fight at.
zones.note: The second of the four PvP zones, and the one with a war going on in it: Longbow and Arachnos armies fight over hotspots, and whichever faction controls the zone unlocks temporary powers from its merchant. Bounty pays you for hunting player enemies. Heroes enter through the Steel Canyon gate on the eastern war wall.

events.name: Battle for Siren's Call
events.note: Longbow vs Arachnos at hotspots — controls the zone

events.name: Bounty
events.note: hunt player enemies for spendable points

zones.pvp_note: FREE-FOR-ALL, not hero-versus-villain: any player may attack any other player here, including your own side. Any level may enter and everyone is set to combat level 38. Shown at 38 because that is the level you actually fight at.
zones.note: Marshal Blitz took the island and started firing warheads at Paragon City; Longbow and Arachnos are both here trying to stop him, and neither trusts the other. The Missile Launch mission is the draw — launching the Warburg Rocket ten times earns Rocketman. The WEB is the Council's old underground weapons labs.

events.name: Missile Launch
events.note: launch the Warburg Rocket — ten launches earns Rocketman

exploration_badges: Weapon Inspector / Weapon of Mass Destruction

zones.since: Issue 9 (villains only at first; became co-op later)
zones.note: An archipelago of abandoned genetic and biological experiments that the Devouring Earth moved into after the humans left. Hamidon appears in the sinkhole; while it is there it is the ONLY enemy in the zone. When it is away, Thorn, Stropharia, Lattice and Quarry monsters roam instead, and killing them earns Essence of the Earth inspirations. One way in only: the Smuggler's Submarine.

events.name: Hamidon
events.note: in the northern bowl, surrounded by its mitochondria

zones.not_pvp_note: Co-op trial zone, NOT a PvP zone. The four PvP zones are Bloody Bay, Siren's Call, Warburg and Recluse's Victory.
zones.team: The zone's own recon officer suggests 5 to 8 people, and says 'The Abyss is no place to wander alone'.
zones.safe_havens_note: the recon officer calls it 'probably the only one'

zones.alt_start_note: Praetoria is a SEPARATE STARTING PATH, not a side trip. A character created here levels through Praetoria instead of Paragon City and is neither hero nor villain until they leave — so the hero and villain routes above do not apply to a Praetorian until then.
zones.note: Cole's showcase district: Cole Tower, the Magisterium, and a police presence that is the point rather than the backdrop. Your contacts split by allegiance — Resistance (Warden arc) or Loyalist (Power and Responsibility arcs) — and that choice is the story. Bridges and the Cole Transportation Authority lead to Imperial City; sewer covers lead to the Underground; a manhole in the Magisterium goes to Pocket D.
contacts: Robert Flores / Tunnel Rat / Doctor Arvin (Resistance, Warden arc)
Deputy Assistant of Information / Warrant / Reese (Loyalist, Power arc)
Chief Interrogator Washington / Cleopatra (Loyalist, Responsibility arc)

zones.alt_start_note: The second leg of the Praetorian path, not a Paragon City zone. Continues from Nova Praetoria; Neutropolis follows.
zones.note: The entertainment and commercial hub — TPN's tower, the Aureas theatre district, People's Park where Cole beat back the Devouring Earth. The Behavioral Adjustment Facility is here, holding Tellurian Plague victims, and the Syndicate runs the corruption the PPD would rather you did not see. Your Resistance-or-Loyalist choice keeps splitting the contacts.
contacts: Jessica Flores / Luke Larson / Dr. Steffard / Seer 1381 (Resistance, Warden arc)
Mr. G / Transmuter / Tami Baker / Praetor Sinclair (Loyalist, Power arc)
Interrogator Kang / Alec Parson / Investigator Whitworth / Chance McKnight (Loyalist, Responsibility arc)

zones.alt_start_note: The last leg of the Praetorian path. At 20 a Praetorian leaves for Primal Earth and picks a side — from there the hero or villain route above takes over.
zones.note: Praetoria's industrial back end: three antimatter reactors, Clockwork foundries, and the last of First Ward's refugees living among the fumes. Ghouls hunt from the manholes and the government is not curious about the missing. Two names here become Incarnate trials later — Lambda Sector and the Keyes Island Reactor.
contacts: Aaron Walker / Penelope Yin / Dark Watcher (Resistance, Warden arc)
IVy / Mother Mayhem / Anti-Matter (Loyalist, Responsibility arc)

zones.since: Issue 0 (the Council Base and War Machine arrived in Issue 25)
zones.art_key_why: The client ships this zone's map as 'Baumton', its pre-Rikti name — the wiki's own overview opens 'the once peaceful burgh of Baumton became…', which is what makes the link sourced rather than assumed. This is the exact historical-name trap flagged when the art was extracted; it is closed here for this zone only, with its reason recorded.
zones.note: Baumton after the Rikti levelled it: toppled skyscrapers, smoking craters, and a stalemate the PPD fights from behind a wall. A hazard zone, so the spawns are bigger than the street outside. In from Steel Canyon's north gate or up from the Sewer Network.

events.name: Babbage
events.note: giant monster, along the northern wall

events.name: War Machine
events.note: the Council's fortified base in the south-east, behind mass-produced War Walkers

plaque_badges: Just Said No to Superadine IV

zones.since: Rebuilt in Issue 8 (the pre-revamp zone is Echo: Faultline)
zones.art_key_why: The client ships this zone's map as 'Overbrook', the town that stood here before the archvillain Faultline destroyed it — the wiki states 'The ruins of Overbrook adopted his name', which sources the link. Second instance of the historical-name case after Boomtown/Baumton; same rule, declared in the data with its reason, never inferred.
zones.note: Gulbasaur's guide already routes you here at 15 and calls it where the modern game begins — this entry adds what his guide does not carry: which parts of it suit which level. The Overbrook Medical Center has no enemies at all.
zones.safe_havens_note: marked Green with no enemies at all
plaque_badges: Just Said No to Superadine V

zones.pvp_note: Other players can attack you here. ANY level may enter and everyone is set to combat level 50. This is the fourth and highest of the PvP zones.
zones.note: An alternate future of Atlas Park where Arachnos and Longbow fight over Temporal Anchors. Capture a pillbox by clearing its four turrets and holding the panel; six captures wins the zone for your side and the landscape itself changes to match. 1,000 zone points before the reversion earns you a Heavy mech. Worth knowing even if you never fight here: simply ENTERING grants Entrusted with the Secret and the Ouroboros Portal outright.

events.name: Temporal Anchors
events.note: capture pillboxes to hold the timeline

events.name: Signature Hero/Villain Reinforcements
events.note: spawn when a side takes its fourth pillbox

events.name: Temporal Reversion
events.note: five minutes after a side wins, everything resets to neutral

contacts: D.A.T.A. Orientation Adjutant / Commander Murat / D.A.T.A. Tech Congreves (hero)
Arachnos Orientation Adjutant / Arbiter Minos / Arbiter Uller (villain)

zones.art_key_why: The client ships this zone's map as 'Woodvale', the suburb that stood here before the Devouring Earth took it. The wiki sources it directly: 'Once, Eden was a beautiful suburb of Paragon City called Woodvale.' Third instance of the historical-name case after Boomtown/Baumton and Faultline/Overbrook — declared with its reason, never inferred.
zones.note: Woodvale after the Devouring Earth buried it: a sprawling wilderness where nature is finishing the job. Woodsman's spirit runs the Eden Trial from here, and the trial's second mission entrance is on the slopes of the largest hive spire beside him. Gates west to The Hive and east to Founders' Falls.

_verification.method: Every exploration badge each wiki page lists was matched against badges.bin, exported from the LIVE game client. A zone whose badges are all present is content that exists in the game today; a zone whose badges are absent would be a stale wiki page and must not ship.
_verification.result: 119 of 119 listed exploration badges found in the live client, across all 14 zones.
_verification.note: This check also caught three transcription errors of mine: the client names those badges Hangman/woman, Burgermeister/meisterin and Longshoreman/woman, and the game strings now win over the wiki summary formatting.
_verification.art_rule: Zone art is served only when its asset name is corroborated as a current zone (badges.bin key, or an art_key a zone declares with a written reason). Withheld: Woodvale, Venice, White Plains, white.
